D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Verdigris — Copper + Vinegar Green

Cu(CH₃COO)₂ scraped off corroded copper sheets gives a brilliant teal-green; Statue-of-Liberty patina is the slow-cousin version

Medieval recipes: bury copper sheets in horse dung soaked in wine vinegar; scrape off green crust monthly. The pigment was loved for its intensity but feared for its instability — it darkens in oil and damages parchment over decades.

Age verdigris over centuries to see fugitive darkening.
